name: Salal
description_short: NCUA-insured credit union headquartered in Seattle, Washington, serving 40,000+ members with $656.18M in assets. Notable for pioneering cannabis industry banking services.
description_long:
Salal Credit Union was founded in Seattle in 1948 as a not-for-profit financial institution by Group Health Cooperative employees, originally named Group Health Credit Union. For decades it operated as an employer-sponsored credit union before expanding membership criteria in 1996 to serve Capitol Hill residents geographically. In 2003, the credit union opened its charter to serve anyone living or working in Washington State, and in 2010 rebranded to Salal Credit Union, named after the Pacific Northwest salal plant. The organization is regulated by the NCUA and maintains membership in the credit union system.

Salal offers standard banking services including checking accounts, savings products, and ATM access to its member base. The credit union also provides indirect lending services to members in other states through a business lending program. A defining feature is their pioneering work in cannabis industry banking—beginning in July 2014, Salal began servicing cannabis businesses when most financial institutions refused due to federal restrictions. By 2022, they were serving approximately 270 cannabis industry customers.

What distinguishes Salal is its willingness to serve the cannabis industry when it faced a critical banking access gap following Washington State legalization in 2012. As of 2017, Salal was one of only five financial institutions in Washington accepting cannabis deposits, and management indicated cannabis company checking accounts represented up to 80 percent of the credit union's net worth. This niche positioning reflects both regulatory courage and community commitment. Additionally, Salal created the Salal Foundation in 2013, a 501(c)(3) organization supporting charitable and educational causes in their service communities.

Salal remains a mid-sized regional credit union that has successfully expanded from employer-based membership to geographic and statewide accessibility. However, their concentration in cannabis banking creates operational and reputational complexity—the industry remains federally illegal despite state legalization, exposing the credit union to regulatory scrutiny and compliance costs. Their service is regionally limited to Washington State (except through indirect lending), and membership growth depends on geographic expansion approvals. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: ATM access and cash withdrawal services
Business checking for cannabis industry clients
Indirect lending programs across multiple states
Deposit accounts for cannabis industry customers
Membership to Washington State residents and workers
Community charitable grants through Salal Foundation
pros: NCUA insurance protection on deposits up to $250,000 per account
Open membership to anyone living or working in Washington State as of 2003
Pioneering cannabis industry banking services—serving 270+ cannabis businesses by 2022
Not-for-profit structure means earnings returned to members rather than external shareholders
Operates Salal Foundation with 501(c)(3) status for community charitable support
Assets of $656.18 million provide stability and liquidity
Indirect lending program extends services to members in other states
Located in Seattle with physical branch access for local members
cons: Limited geographic service area—primarily Washington State unless using indirect lending program
Heavy concentration in cannabis industry banking (up to 80% of net worth per 2017 reporting) creates regulatory and reputational risk
Cannabis banking subjects credit union to elevated federal scrutiny despite state legalization
Smaller institution relative to major banks—may lack some advanced digital features or product breadth
Member growth dependent on charter expansion approval from regulators
best_for: Washington State residents seeking NCUA-insured credit union membership with community focus
Cannabis industry businesses needing banking services when most institutions refuse them
Members prioritizing not-for-profit financial institutions over shareholder-owned banks
Customers wanting connection to institution actively supporting local charitable causes
diagnosis: Salal is best for Washington State residents seeking a credit union with community values and NCUA protection, or cannabis industry businesses facing institutional banking rejection. Primary caveat: their business model depends significantly on cannabis industry banking (up to 80% of net worth), which remains federally illegal and exposes the institution to regulatory risk and compliance costs that most traditional banks avoid.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
